affects: every run's parallelism, determinism and provenance

runtime._measured: Capping the pools: 12.33 s -> 8.15 s at three workers (1.51x). Worker benchmark over eight tables: 63.90 s / 33.71 / 24.62 / 19.72 wall at jobs 1..4, core-seconds 63.88 / 65.73 / 65.96 / 67.88 — wall falls monotonically while core-seconds rise 6%, so four workers are Pareto-optimal.
runtime._reproducibility_standard: BYTE-LEVEL: a task's result_sha256 is identical across re-runs. The integrity guard (worker._publish) enforces it strictly and is NEVER loosened — a divergent re-run is a real defect, quarantined, not tolerated.
runtime._reproducibility_conditions: Byte-level holds iff ALL of: single-thread numeric pools (above), seeded RNG (seed 42 + per-permutation default_rng), PYTHONHASHSEED=0 (pins str-set iteration order, else per-process order-dependent float accumulation flips a futility exceedance and changes bytes), and NO concurrent duplicate execution of one task (per-task heartbeat prevents the guard from requeuing a live long task).
runtime._reproducibility: Capping does not touch the arithmetic: stage 3 re-run on TDG reproduced [-0.09471459, -0.11062278, +0.04853174, +0.24495527] exactly. 2026-07-23: the max-null verified byte-identical across two clean re-runs on ADBE (near-tie, coarser grid) and AZO (survivor) once PYTHONHASHSEED was pinned — the earlier FAILED_INTEGRITY was a false alarm from an unpinned hash seed under a stale-requeue duplicate, not a scientific non-reproducibility.

cost_model_core_seconds_per_ticker._note: Measured before the runtime fix, at three workers with uncapped pools. Re-measure under the new configuration before budgeting a new panel.

run_manifest_required_fields.execution_head_sha: git HEAD at the moment the run started
run_manifest_required_fields.contract_sha256: sha256 of this file as read by the run
run_manifest_required_fields.code_dirty: whether the working tree carried uncommitted changes
run_manifest_required_fields.environment: python, platform, cpu count, thread pool settings and what the pools actually reported
run_manifest_required_fields.permutations_executed: per candidate, so an early stop is visible
